objectives.text: Listen for specific information and respond appropriately in oral activities.
Speak clearly to describe experiences, retell events, and share ideas.
Ask and answer simple questions to clarify meaning and extend discussion.
Identify letter-sound relationships and use them to decode simple words.
Blend and segment sounds in one- and two-syllable words.

Explain how people, plants, and animals depend on each other.
Use simple inquiry and project work to solve familiar real-world problems.
Identify habits that support personal health, hygiene, and safety.
Demonstrate positive social skills and respectful relationships.
Discuss feelings, choices, and behaviours that promote well-being.
Read texts independently and identify main ideas, supporting details, and purpose.
Write clear paragraphs and short compositions for different purposes.
Use grammar, spelling, and punctuation more accurately in oral and written communication.

objectives.text: Solve algebraic, geometric, trigonometric, and graphical problems accurately.

Write organized responses for informative, persuasive, and literary purposes.
Use language appropriately for audience, purpose, and context.

Analyze ecological relationships using data, diagrams, and real-world examples.

topics.name: Cells, Nutrition, Respiration, Transport, and Health

objectives.text: Explain major life processes and the structures that support them.
Interpret biological data and experiments related to life processes.
Apply biological knowledge to health and disease contexts.

frameworks.subject: Social Studies
frameworks.topic_group: Sustainable Development and Use of Resources

objectives.text: Explain sustainable development and the use of resources in the Caribbean.
Interpret information about development, resources, and regional issues.
Evaluate policies and actions related to resource use and regional growth.

Evaluate sporting situations using fair play and performance criteria.
